slides.title: Ternary for Inline Conditions ❓
slides.content:
# The Ternary Operator

The ternary operator `condition ? exprIfTrue : exprIfFalse` is an expression, so it can be used inside JSX.

## Syntax

```jsx
{isLoggedIn ? <LogoutButton /> : <LoginButton />}
```

It's perfect when you need to choose between two things to render.

slides.title: Ternary in Action
slides.content: Here's a component that shows a different message:

quizData.question:
What will the following render if `isAdmin` is false? ```jsx
{isAdmin ? 'Admin' : 'User'}
```
quizData.explanation: The ternary returns 'User' when condition is false, so it renders 'User' inside the div.
